title: 36" Floating Vanity Cabinet
summary: A wall-hung vanity cabinet with no legs at all — lag-bolted directly to studs, with vertical dividers you cut into on-site to clear your actual vanity top and sink.
description:
This vanity carries its entire weight on lag screws into wall studs, not on legs or a toe-kick base — a genuinely floating cabinet, sized here for a 22" deep by 37" wide vanity top but adjustable to fit others. Pocket-hole the bottom to the sides first, then attach the 1x3 top trim with its nicest edge facing forward, since the pocket holes here will be covered by the vanity top later anyway.

**The back pieces aren't decorative — they're the main structural support** that carries the cabinet's hanging load, so use extra pocket holes and glue rather than treating them as an afterthought. Before permanently installing the vertical dividers, **set the actual vanity top and sink in place and mark for any cutout needed** — this build required cutting into the tops of the dividers with a jigsaw to clear the sink, and that fit has to be checked against your real hardware, not the plan's stated dimensions.

Shelves aren't structural, so they're glued and nailed rather than pocket-holed, keeping the interior free of visible screws. **Hang the cabinet with 4" lag screws spaced as far apart vertically as possible** — one near the top, one near the bottom — since each lag screw needs to support a minimum of 100 lbs on its own for the cabinet to carry real everyday use safely.

tools.note: For cutting the vertical dividers to clear the sink.

materials.name: Euro hinges, inset for frameless cabinets, pair
Lag screws, 4", for hanging to studs

cutList.note: Place the nicest edge outward on the front.
May need cutting to clear your actual sink.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the bottom and attach to the sides, pocket holes hidden on the bottom. Attach the 1x3 top trim, pocket holes hidden on top, with the nicest edge facing forward.

steps.title: Attach the back supports
steps.body: Attach the back support panels, which carry the cabinet's hanging load, with extra pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Test-fit the sink and vanity top
steps.body: Place the vanity top and sink on the cabinet before installing the vertical dividers, and mark any cutout needed. Cut into the tops of the dividers with a jigsaw if the sink requires it.

steps.title: Install the dividers and back supports
steps.body: Measure and cut the back support boards to fit, attach in place with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue. Install the vertical dividers, using the 8" shelf piece as a spacing guide.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelves with glue and brad nails to keep the interior free of visible pocket holes or screws.

steps.title: Hang the cabinet
steps.body: Hang the cabinet to studs in the wall using the 4" lag screws, spaced as far apart vertically as possible for maximum support.
materials: Lag screws, 4", for hanging to studs

steps.title: Finish and install the door
steps.body: Edge-band the exposed plywood edges, sand, fill, and finish with cabinet grade paint, then install the door with the euro hinges.
materials: Euro hinges, inset for frameless cabinets, pair
